solutions.solution: Taking digits-sum of given numbers. Input: 53 47 25 17 68. Explanation: 53 gives 5 + 3 = 8, 11 + 68 gives 6 + 8 = 14 and so on. Ex 7: Taking digit-sum repeatedly. Input: 83 47 25 17 68. Step I: 14 13 7 8 14. Explanation: 83 gives 8 + 3 = 11, 81 gives 1 + 1 = 2... and so on. Starting with given digit-sum (... and may be adding or subtracting number too). Ex 8: Squaring digit-sum. Input: 83 47 25 17 68. Step I: 121 25 49 64 196. Explanation: 83 gives 8 + 3 = 11, 11² = 121... Ex 9: Cubing digit-sum and adding 50. Input: 19 4 18 29 6. Step I: 1000 114 611 1100 1266. Explanation: 19 gives 1 + 9 = 10, 10³ = 1000, 1000 + 50 = 1050, 29 gives 2+9=11, 11³ = 1331, 1331 + 50 = 1381... Adding or subtracting the square or cubes of natural numbers. Ex 10: Adding squares (1², 2², 3²...) of natural numbers to the given number. Input: 14 17 12 10 9. Step I: 15 21 21 26 34. Explanation: 14 + 1², 17 + 2², 12 + 3²... Ex 11: Subtracting cubes of natural numbers from given numbers. Input: 171 69 481 95 168. Step I: 170 61 454 31 43. Explanation: (171 - 1³, 69 - 2³, 481 - 3³...). Note: There can be many more cases. The above is just an indication that the reader is advised to apply himself while solving the problem.
